title: From Hollywood Makeup Artist to Clean Beauty Pioneer: Laura Linsenmeyer's Story
guestBio: Laura Linsenmeyer is a clean beauty pioneer and founder of ROOTS the Beauty Underground, based in Laguna Beach. A former film and TV makeup artist, she transitioned from Hollywood to entrepreneurship to create a purpose-driven, non-toxic beauty brand that champions community and conscious living.
excerpt: Discover how Laura Linsenmeyer left her Hollywood makeup career to build ROOTS the Beauty Underground, a clean beauty brand that's redefining what it means to lead with purpose. This episode reveals the courage it takes to build a conscious business on your own terms.
metaDescription: Clean beauty entrepreneur Laura Linsenmeyer shares how she built ROOTS Beauty from Hollywood roots, balancing motherhood and mission-driven leadership.
keyTakeaways: Transitioning from a creative career to entrepreneurship requires courage to follow your values over security.
Clean beauty isn't just about products—it's about creating a movement toward conscious living.
Building a purpose-driven business means prioritizing community and values alongside profit.
Motherhood can amplify your mission and drive you to create positive change in the world.
Leading a woman-powered brand requires intentional cultivation of supportive relationships.
Authenticity in business comes from aligning your personal values with your professional mission.
Success in conscious entrepreneurship is measured by impact, not just revenue.

pullQuotes.text: Clean beauty isn't just about what we put on our skin—it's about the values we choose to live by
When you lead from the inside out, your business becomes an extension of who you are
Building a conscious brand means choosing courage over comfort every single day

content:
What does it take to leave the glittering world of Hollywood makeup artistry and dive headfirst into the conscious beauty movement? According to Laura Linsenmeyer, founder of ROOTS the Beauty Underground, it takes a combination of courage, conviction, and an unwavering commitment to your values. In this powerful episode of The Manage Her, host Aimee Rickabus sits down with Laura to explore the intersection of entrepreneurship, motherhood, and mission-driven leadership.

Laura's story begins in the fast-paced world of film and television, where she worked as a makeup artist creating looks for celebrities and productions. While the work was creative and exciting, she began to question the products she was using daily—not just their impact on her clients' skin, but their broader environmental and health implications. This awakening wasn't sudden; it was a gradual shift that eventually became impossible to ignore.

The Leap from Security to Purpose

Making the transition from a steady career in entertainment to launching her own beauty brand required Laura to confront her deepest fears about security and success. You know that feeling when you're comfortable but not fulfilled—Laura lived in that space for years before deciding that alignment with her values mattered more than a predictable paycheck. Her decision to launch ROOTS wasn't just about creating another beauty brand; it was about pioneering a movement toward conscious consumption and non-toxic living.

The early days of building ROOTS required Laura to become comfortable with uncertainty while staying true to her vision. She discovered that entrepreneurship isn't just about having a great product idea—it's about having the resilience to weather the inevitable storms that come with building something from scratch. Every decision, from sourcing ingredients to packaging design, became an opportunity to reinforce her commitment to clean beauty principles.

Redefining Clean Beauty

Throughout the conversation, Laura challenges the conventional understanding of what clean beauty actually means. It's not just about avoiding harmful chemicals or using organic ingredients—though those elements are certainly important. For Laura, clean beauty represents a holistic approach to wellness that encompasses environmental responsibility, ethical sourcing, and community building. It's about creating products that honor both the people who use them and the planet that provides the raw materials.

This philosophy extends to every aspect of how ROOTS operates as a business. Laura emphasizes that being a conscious brand means making decisions that might not always be the most profitable in the short term but align with long-term values and sustainability. She's learned that customers who care about clean beauty are also customers who care about how businesses treat their employees, their communities, and their environmental impact.

The conversation reveals how Laura has navigated the challenges of educating consumers about clean beauty while building a profitable business. She's discovered that authenticity resonates more than perfection—customers want to see the real journey, including the mistakes and learning experiences that come with building a purpose-driven company.

Community as the Foundation

One of the most striking aspects of Laura's approach to business is her emphasis on community building. Rather than viewing ROOTS simply as a product company, she's created a platform for like-minded women to connect around shared values of wellness and conscious living. This community-first approach has become a cornerstone of her business strategy and a source of personal fulfillment.

Laura shares how being based in Laguna Beach has influenced her understanding of community and tradition. The area's emphasis on environmental consciousness and holistic wellness has provided both inspiration and a testing ground for her products and values. She's learned that building a local community first creates a strong foundation for broader growth and impact.

The discussion touches on how Laura has cultivated relationships with other female entrepreneurs and business owners, creating a network of support that extends far beyond traditional business partnerships. She's discovered that leading a woman-powered brand means intentionally creating space for other women to thrive, whether they're employees, customers, or fellow business owners.

Motherhood as a Leadership Amplifier

Perhaps the most personal part of the conversation focuses on how motherhood has shaped Laura's approach to leadership and business building. Rather than seeing her role as a mother as something that competes with her entrepreneurial ambitions, she's found that motherhood has amplified her sense of purpose and urgency around creating positive change.

Laura explains how becoming a mother made her even more committed to creating non-toxic products and promoting conscious living. The desire to leave a better world for her children has become a driving force behind every business decision. This perspective has also influenced how she thinks about the legacy she wants to build with ROOTS—not just as a successful company, but as a catalyst for broader change in the beauty industry.

The conversation explores the practical realities of balancing motherhood with running a growing business. Laura is refreshingly honest about the challenges while also highlighting the unique strengths that mothers bring to entrepreneurship—the ability to multitask, prioritize effectively, and maintain perspective on what truly matters.

Leading from the Inside Out

Throughout the episode, a central theme emerges around what it means to lead from the inside out. For Laura, this means ensuring that her personal values and business practices are in complete alignment, even when that alignment requires difficult choices or sacrifices. It's about creating a business that feels like an authentic expression of who she is rather than a separate entity she manages.

This approach to leadership extends to how Laura treats her team and customers. She's committed to creating a work environment that reflects her values around wellness, community, and purpose. The result is a company culture that attracts people who are genuinely passionate about the mission, creating a positive feedback loop that strengthens both the business and its impact.

Laura's story offers a roadmap for anyone considering the leap from traditional employment to purpose-driven entrepreneurship. Her journey demonstrates that building a conscious business requires courage, persistence, and an unwavering commitment to your values—but the rewards extend far beyond financial success to include personal fulfillment and positive impact on the world around you.
